hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/TM5 while reducing off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong binding but not sufficient selectivity. The focus must shift to explicitly disrupting off-target interactions at conserved positions while preserving the pharmacophore.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) that are likely responsible for off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
Introducing charged or small hydrophobic residues at these positions can break conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the pharmacophore.
